mechanism_narrative: A stable or declining FEDFUNDS path lowers the equity discount rate and reduces recession risk priced by investors, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is strongest when inflation is not accelerating, because easing then reads as supportive rather than reactive.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher after flat-to-lower FEDFUNDS observations than after rising FEDFUNDS observations for two consecutive rolling 3-year windows, reject the mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals weakening household income and earnings risk, which depresses equities over the next month. This negative effect is clearest when PAYEMS is also decelerating, indicating labor-market deterioration rather than a one-off survey noise spike.
failure_condition: Reject if triggered episodes do not show lower median 20-day SPX returns than non-triggered episodes, or if the sign turns positive in more than 45% of triggers over any 4-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When RSAFS and HOUST both weaken together, the market reads a synchronized demand slowdown in consumption and housing, which reduces cyclical earnings expectations and pulls the S&P 500 lower over the next 60 trading days. The joint condition matters because either series alone can be noisy, but concurrent weakness identifies broad domestic demand loss.
failure_condition: Reject if the interaction term adds no incremental explanatory power versus each series alone, or if simultaneous RSAFS and HOUST declines fail to precede below-median 60-day SPX returns in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A falling INDPRO indicates weakening industrial activity and softer real-side momentum, which tends to precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days because investors reprice cyclicals before earnings revisions arrive. The signal should be strongest when production is below its recent trend rather than merely volatile.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO falls below its 3-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if below-trend INDPRO periods do not produce lower 5-day forward returns than above-trend periods, or if the effect disappears in more than half of rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% tightens financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism fails when inflation is falling fast, because markets then anticipate imminent policy easing and discount-rate pressure eases.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS trigger periods do not exhibit lower median 60-day SPX returns than lower-rate periods, or if the signal reverses when CPI inflation is declining.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in GDP and retail sales signals a genuine broad demand contraction, not just an inventory or sector-specific slowdown, and that combination should precede negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The claim is falsified if either series worsens alone but the joint deterioration does not.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDP-and-sales weakness does not outperform single-series signals in predicting 60-day SPX downside, or if positive forward returns dominate after the joint decline pattern.

mechanism_narrative: In a labor-market cooling phase, PAYEMS softening and UNRATE rising jointly indicate weakening wage income and future consumption, which should translate into lower S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal is not active when unemployment rises but payroll growth remains strong, because then the labor market is not truly deteriorating.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined PAYEMS/UNRATE pattern fails to produce lower 20-day returns than either series alone, or if the sign is positive in a majority of cooling episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in HOUST after a housing trough signals improving construction activity and a coming rebound in cyclically sensitive earnings, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The effect should appear only in post-downturn recoveries, not during late-cycle housing booms.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises above its 6-month average after a prior 3-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound triggers do not precede above-median 60-day SPX returns, or if the effect persists equally in boom and recovery regimes.
